description_long: This object is a traditional pickaxe with a robust, dark wooden handle and a double-sided, curved metal head. The handle is cylindrical and appears to be made from a dense hardwood, showing a natural wood grain and a slightly worn finish. The head is forged from dark metal, with two pointed, slightly curved prongs extending symmetrically from a central hub that secures it to the handle. The metal head has a matte, slightly rough surface, suggesting durability and resistance to wear. The overall construction is solid and utilitarian, suitable for heavy-duty tasks such as breaking up hard ground or concrete. There are no visible logos, markings, or mounting hardware on the handle or head.
description: A sturdy pickaxe with a dark wooden handle and a double-sided, curved metal head, designed for heavy-duty digging or breaking tasks.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the curved metal head and the straight wooden handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the metal head and the cylindrical profile of the handle.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the symmetrical prongs of the metal head and the handle's grain.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the head's thickness and the handle's round shape.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle with a natural finish and no visible grip enhancements.
attribute_blade_type: Double-sided, curved pickaxe head with pointed prongs and a central hub; matte dark metal with no visible reinforcement.
